page_title: Paid family and sick leave — New Hampshire
meta_title: New Hampshire Paid Family and Sick Leave 2026 | GSPFL + FMLA Guide | Teamed
meta_description: New Hampshire has no mandatory state paid leave for private employers. Federal FMLA at 50+ employees gives 12 weeks unpaid. The GSPFL opt-in plan gives 6 weeks at 60% via MetLife.
target_keywords: granite state paid family leave plan

hero_image.alt: Aerial view of New Hampshire White Mountains autumn foliage, forested hillsides in deep red, orange, and gold stretching to the horizon under a clear blue sky.
hero_image.query: New Hampshire White Mountains autumn foliage aerial landscape

entity_badge: Served by Teamed partner network: Suna Solutions, Inc. (primary US partner)

factual_uncertainty_flags.note: GSPFL premium schedule, current participation rates, and plan terms should be verified with NH Insurance Department before client-facing use. Programme may have changed since 1 January 2023 launch.
factual_uncertainty_flags.action: Verify with NH Insurance Department (nh.gov/insurance) and MetLife before publishing

factual_uncertainty_flags.note: OnPay cites 8.50% max UI rate; NH Payroll Guide cites 7.00% for experience-rated employers. Not quoted on this page (covered in sibling state-income-tax page).
